summary: Returns paginated award or subaward search rows for a filtered advanced-search population, with live award-mode probing confirming the default spending_level=awards plus helper identifiers such as generated_internal_id; this bundle exposes the response-side semantic fact canonical_award_lookup_id and normalizes it for downstream award drilldowns.
businessPurpose: Use this endpoint when you need to inspect the actual award or subaward records behind a complex advanced-search filter set. It is the row-level drilldown surface for the broader USAspending advanced-search system.
analyticalGrain: One result row per matching prime award by default, or one result row per matching subaward when spending_level=subawards or subawards=true.

primaryEntities.name: Prime award row
primaryEntities.description: A single filtered award returned in award mode with requested display columns plus helper identifiers such as internal_id and generated_internal_id.

primaryEntities.name: Subaward row
primaryEntities.description: A single filtered subaward returned in subaward mode with requested subaward labels plus prime-award linkage helpers.

primaryEntities.name: Advanced-search filter population
primaryEntities.description: The underlying set of awards or subawards selected by AdvancedFilterObject before projection and pagination are applied.

measures.name: Award Amount
measures.description: Prime-award monetary amount available for supported award families when requested. In bounded high-value screens, this remains the award-level amount on each returned row rather than a period-only subtotal.

measures.name: Total Outlays
measures.description: Prime-award outlay measure available for supported award families when requested.

measures.name: Loan Value and Subsidy Cost
measures.description: Loan/assistance measures available on the relevant award families when requested.

measures.name: Sub-Award Amount
measures.description: Subaward monetary amount available in subaward mode when requested.

measures.name: DEFC-specific obligation and outlay fields
measures.description: COVID-19 and infrastructure obligation/outlay fields can appear when requested and when the underlying row has matching DEFC data.

dimensions.name: Award versus subaward mode
dimensions.description: spending_level and subawards determine whether results represent prime awards or subawards.

dimensions.name: Requested field catalog
dimensions.description: The fields array controls which business columns are projected into each row and therefore shapes the response schema.

dimensions.name: Advanced search filters
dimensions.description: The shared AdvancedFilterObject lets callers slice by time, agencies, recipient/location criteria, award types, program filters, NAICS/PSC/TAS, and disaster codes before row retrieval.

dimensions.name: Award family
dimensions.description: Contracts, IDVs, loans, non-loan assistance, and subaward families expose different valid field labels and measures.

dimensions.name: Canonical award lookup id
dimensions.description: For prime-award rows, this bundle exposes results[].canonical_award_lookup_id as an explicit response-side semantic fact derived from results[].generated_internal_id, even when the visible fields array stays small; reuse that value unchanged when chaining into v2__awards__award_id or v2__awards__funding, where award detail may echo the same business key as generated_unique_award_id.

suitableQuestions.name: Which awards match this complex filter set?
suitableQuestions.description: Best for browsing actual award rows behind advanced search criteria instead of just counts or aggregates.

suitableQuestions.name: Which contract awards are highest-value in this filtered slice?
suitableQuestions.description: Use the bounded high_value_award_screening pattern with Award Amount descending to surface top-dollar contract candidates active in the filtered slice, then capture the explicit response-side handoff fact canonical_award_lookup_id before drilling into award detail or funding workflows. Verify award dates before describing hits as newly signed awards, because the bounded time_period can surface older long-running awards and Award Amount remains an award-level field.

suitableQuestions.name: Who are the top recipients or agencies in a filtered slice?
suitableQuestions.description: Request recipient, agency, and amount fields, then sort and paginate through the matching rows.

suitableQuestions.name: Which subawards sit under a filtered prime-award population?
suitableQuestions.description: Switch to spending_level=subawards and request subaward labels plus prime-award linkage fields.

suitableQuestions.name: Which award rows should I inspect further in award detail APIs?
suitableQuestions.description: Use this endpoint to identify candidate rows and capture the response-side semantic fact canonical_award_lookup_id for downstream award detail or funding lookups.

notSuitableFor.name: Ready-made aggregates or totals
notSuitableFor.description: This endpoint returns rows, not grouped summaries. Use aggregate endpoints when the question is about totals by geography, time, or award type.

notSuitableFor.name: Bulk export of very large result sets
notSuitableFor.description: Deep pagination becomes a sequential search_after workflow and may be a poor fit for full-population extraction.

notSuitableFor.name: Earlier-than-search-floor historical coverage
notSuitableFor.description: The shared search system can emit warnings about the earliest searchable date and may require download workflows for older history.

notSuitableFor.name: Questions that need fields you did not request
notSuitableFor.description: The response is projection-driven, so omitted columns are absent rather than implicitly available.

joins.description: Treat this explicit response-side semantic fact as the canonical award handoff key. In prime-award search results it is derived from generated_internal_id; pass it unchanged to v2__awards__award_id path award_id or v2__awards__funding body award_id, and expect award detail to echo the same business key as generated_unique_award_id.

generated_internal_id is the transport-level helper that backs the response-side semantic fact canonical_award_lookup_id. When you need a downstream award drilldown key, read or normalize the value as canonical_award_lookup_id, then pass the same string unchanged to v2__awards__award_id path award_id or v2__awards__funding body award_id.
When Awarding Agency is requested, rows may also include agency_slug that can support agency-profile linking when a profile page exists.

Subaward mode returns prime-award linkage helpers; when present, treat prime_award_generated_internal_id as the prime-award canonical_award_lookup_id for downstream prime-award detail or funding workflows.

workflows.name: Screen for high-value contract awards
workflows.description: Use the high-value contract screening template to surface top-dollar contract candidates quickly, then carry the explicit response-side handoff fact canonical_award_lookup_id from the chosen row into downstream detail workflows. Interpret the bounded time_period as an activity screen, not as a guarantee that returned awards were newly signed in that period.

steps.action: Start from the high_value_award_screening request shape: contract award_type_codes A/B/C/D, a bounded time_period, page=1, limit=5, and the small visible field set Award ID / Recipient Name / Award Amount / Awarding Agency.
Sort by Award Amount in descending order so the first page surfaces top-dollar contract candidates rather than alphabetical Award ID rows.

Capture canonical_award_lookup_id from the selected award row as the explicit response-side semantic fact derived from generated_internal_id, even though that helper identifier is not part of the requested visible fields.

Before describing a hit as a new award in the bounded period, verify date_signed or period-of-performance dates in award detail: a bounded time_period screen can surface older long-running awards that are active in the period, while Award Amount remains an award-level value on the returned row.

Reuse canonical_award_lookup_id unchanged in downstream award drilldowns: send it as path award_id to v2__awards__award_id or as body award_id to v2__awards__funding; award detail may echo the same value as generated_unique_award_id.

workflows.name: Preview awards before detailed follow-up
workflows.description: Build a narrow filter set, request only the columns you need, then carry canonical_award_lookup_id from the selected row into downstream detail workflows.

steps.action: Assemble the AdvancedFilterObject for the slice you want to inspect.
Choose a small fields array plus sort/page/limit controls so the response schema stays interpretable and bounded.

Use the explicit response-side semantic fact canonical_award_lookup_id for downstream award detail or funding workflows; in prime-award mode it is derived from generated_internal_id, and in subaward mode use prime_award_generated_internal_id for the linked prime award when present.

workflows.name: Sequential deep pagination
workflows.description: For deep result windows, replay the same request with the page_metadata cursor pair rather than assuming simple random page jumps remain valid.

steps.action: Request a bounded page and inspect page_metadata.hasNext plus the optional last_record cursor pair.
If another page is needed, send the same filters, fields, sort, and order together with both last_record_unique_id and last_record_sort_value.

caveats.statement: The response schema is caller-shaped by the fields array and varies across award families and subaward mode.
caveats.impact: Downstream code must not assume one universal row schema for all requests.

caveats.statement: Some requested labels return nested objects rather than simple scalars.
caveats.impact: Schema-on-read logic should handle object-valued cells such as NAICS, PSC, and location fields.

caveats.statement: The legacy subawards boolean and the spending_level selector both influence row grain.
caveats.impact: Be explicit about the desired mode so prime-award and subaward templates are not mixed accidentally.

caveats.statement: Live responses can warn that search start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.
caveats.impact: For older historical work, plan a download workflow rather than assuming this search surface covers the full 2000-forward window.

caveats.statement: Related award drilldown endpoints relabel the same handoff business key: search emits generated_internal_id, award detail may echo generated_unique_award_id, and funding expects that same value under request field award_id.
caveats.impact: Normalize the handoff value as canonical_award_lookup_id, read it from the bundle's explicit response-side semantic fact when available, and do not substitute the display Award ID when the generated helper is available.

caveats.statement: A bounded time_period screen can return older long-running awards that are active in the filtered period, while Award Amount still reflects the award-level value on each returned row.
caveats.impact: Do not describe high-value hits as newly signed period awards without checking date_signed or period-of-performance fields in downstream award detail.
